title: Unify operator-hours formatting between Data Entry and Reports/Exports

requirements.summary: Make Reports/exports display the exact same Total Operator Hours representation as the Data Entry form for the same production entry.
acceptanceCriteria: After saving a new production entry, the Reports table column “Op Hours” equals the Data Entry form’s displayed “Total Operator Hours” for that entry (no rounding/threshold differences).
CSV export column “Total Operator Hours” and HTML export column “Operator Hrs/Operator Hrs” match the Data Entry form’s displayed “Total Operator Hours” for the same entry.

The backend no longer stores Total Operator Hours as whole hours only when the Data Entry form would show minutes/seconds for the same entry (i.e., stored hours/minutes/seconds match the form’s computed breakdown).

file_operations.description: Add a shared Total Operator Hours formatting helper that accepts either number-based or bigint-based time intervals and produces a single canonical display string consistent with Reports/exports (so Data Entry and Reports can render identical values for the same {hours,minutes,seconds}).

Replace the Data Entry form’s Total Operator Hours display to use the shared operator-hours formatter from frontend/src/utils/operatorHours.ts (instead of the current formatTimeInterval output) so the on-form displayed value matches the Reports/exports representation for the same entry.

Update the Reports table “Op Hours” cell and both CSV/HTML export renderers to use the same shared operator-hours formatter from frontend/src/utils/operatorHours.ts (removing/avoiding local duplicate formatting helpers) to guarantee the Reports table and exports match the Data Entry form’s displayed Total Operator Hours for the same entry.
